title: pinguicula vulgaris proles alpicola
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris proles alpicola, also known as the Alpine Butterwort, is a rare and carnivorous perennial plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and iridoids. The health benefits of Pinguicula vulgaris proles alpicola are believed to include wound healing, anti-oxidant, and anti-cancer properties, although further research is required to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, although commercial cultivation and availability may be limited due to its rarity and specific growing conditions.
